# Research Report: Scientifically Validated Breathing Techniques
Generated: 2026-04-02

## Summary
Eight breathing techniques researched with exact timings and scientific backing. The physiological sigh (Stanford 2023) has the strongest recent RCT evidence.

## 1. 4-7-8 Breathing
- Creator: Dr. Andrew Weil (2015), from pranayama
- Inhale: 4s (nose) | Hold-in: 7s | Exhale: 8s (mouth, whoosh) | Hold-out: 0s
- Total cycle: 19s (~3.2 bpm), 4 cycles beginners, up to 8
- Best for: Sleep onset, anxiety, acute stress
- Ratio 4:7:8 matters more than absolute seconds
- Evidence: PMC9277512 HRV study; 2025 scoping review (15 studies); Cleveland Clinic
- Confidence: HIGH

## 2. Box Breathing (Tactical)
- Creator: Mark Divine (Navy SEAL commander)
- Inhale: 4s | Hold-in: 4s | Exhale: 4s | Hold-out: 4s
- Total cycle: 16s (3.75 bpm), 4-5 min sessions
- Best for: Focus, acute stress, pre-performance calm
- Evidence: Ma et al. (2017) Frontiers Psychology; military trauma protocols
- Confidence: HIGH

## 3. Coherent Breathing (Resonant)
- Creator: Stephen Elliott (2005), "The New Science of Breath"
- Inhale: 5.5s (nose) | Hold-in: 0 | Exhale: 5.5s | Hold-out: 0
- Total cycle: 11s (~5.5 bpm), 10-20 min sessions
- Best for: HRV optimization, autonomic balance, blood pressure
- Evidence: PubMed 24380741 maximized HRV; Nature Sci Reports 2023 400-person RCT (BUT primary stress outcome NOT significant vs placebo)
- Confidence: MEDIUM (HRV yes, stress reduction debated)

## 4. Physiological Sigh (Cyclic Sighing)
- Creator: Dr. Andrew Huberman + Dr. David Spiegel (Stanford)
- Inhale 1: 2-3s deep nasal | Inhale 2: 1s short sharp nasal | Hold: 0 | Exhale: 6-8s slow mouth
- Total cycle: ~10-12s, 5 min sessions or 1-3 sighs acute
- Best for: Immediate stress/anxiety, mood, lowering arousal
- Evidence: Balban et al. (2023) Cell Reports Medicine 111-person RCT -- BEAT meditation (p<0.05)
- Confidence: HIGH (strongest recent RCT)

## 5. Diaphragmatic (Belly) Breathing
- Creator: General clinical (Cleveland Clinic, Harvard, VA, Johns Hopkins)
- Inhale: 4s nose (beginners 2-3s) | Hold: 0-2s | Exhale: 6s mouth (beginners 3-4s) | Hold-out: 0
- Total cycle: 10-12s (~5-6 bpm), 5-10 min 3-4x daily
- Best for: Foundation technique, COPD/asthma, relaxation
- Progression: Wk1-2 (2s/3s) > Wk3-4 (3s/4s) > Wk5+ (4s/2s/6s)
- Evidence: PMC7602530 review; decades clinical use
- Confidence: HIGH

## 6. Extended Exhale (4-6 or 4-2-6)
- Creator: General clinical; Calm app, NHS
- Inhale: 4s | Hold: 0-2s | Exhale: 6s | Hold-out: 0
- Total cycle: 10-12s (~5-6 bpm), 5-10 min
- Best for: Anxiety, pre-sleep, vagus nerve activation
- Evidence: Gerritsen & Band (2018) Frontiers Human Neuroscience; exhale-dominant = vagal tone
- Confidence: HIGH

## 7. Wim Hof Breathing
- Creator: Wim Hof
- Phase 1: 30-40 rapid breaths (~1s in, ~1s out)
- Phase 2: Exhale fully, hold out 60-90 seconds
- Phase 3: Deep inhale, hold 15 seconds, release
- 3-4 rounds, ~15-20 min total
- Best for: Energy, cold tolerance, immune modulation
- Evidence: Kox et al. (2014) PNAS sympathetic activation
- APP CAUTION: fainting risk, panic risk, contraindicated pregnancy/epilepsy/heart
- If in app: disclaimer + seated confirm + beginner mode (20 breaths) + no auto-play
- Confidence: MEDIUM

## 8. Additional Techniques

### Alternate Nostril (Nadi Shodhana)
- Left in 4s > Hold 4s > Right out 4s > Hold 4s > Reverse
- Best for: Balance, focus. Study: Ghiya & Lee (2012)

### SKY Breathing (Sudarshan Kriya)
- Multi-phase slow>medium>fast cyclical
- Best for: Depression, PTSD. Study: Zope & Zope (2013)
- Hard to implement in simple timer app

## Comparison Matrix

Technique     | In   | Hold | Out  | Hold | BPM  | Use         | Evidence
4-7-8         | 4s   | 7s   | 8s   | 0    | 3.2  | Sleep       | Moderate
Box           | 4s   | 4s   | 4s   | 4s   | 3.75 | Focus       | Moderate
Coherent      | 5.5s | 0    | 5.5s | 0    | 5.5  | HRV         | Large (mixed)
Phys. Sigh    | 3+1s | 0    | 7s   | 0    | ~5.5 | Acute Stress| Strong
Diaphragmatic | 4s   | 0-2s | 6s   | 0    | 5-6  | Foundation  | Decades
Extended Exh  | 4s   | 0-2s | 6s   | 0    | 5-6  | Anxiety     | Strong
Wim Hof       | rapid| 0    | 60-90| 15s  | Var  | Energy      | Moderate

## App Recommendations
1. Safe 5 (no liability): Diaphragmatic, Box, 4-7-8, Coherent, Physiological Sigh
2. Extended Exhale as default "relax" mode
3. Wim Hof only with safety disclaimers
4. Apple Watch uses 7 bpm default (~4.3s/4.3s)
